visual_notes: One of the most visually variable oxides. Specular hematite is mirror-silver; earthy hematite is brick-red. Red streak unites all forms. Iron roses are among the most dramatic mineral specimens.
prompt_fragments: iron rose rosette from Alpine fissure
vibe_notes: Hematite bridges the prehistoric and the industrial — the first pigment and a global iron ore. Red streak as diagnostic clue is an unforgettable mineralogical moment.
folklore_summary: Name from Greek 'haima' (blood) for the red streak and powder. Ancient amulets believed to stop bleeding. Ochre use predates all other known pigments.
